mechanism_narrative: A higher FEDFUNDS setting tightens discount rates and lifts Treasury term-premium expectations, so the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days when policy is already in a tightening cycle and inflation remains elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS rises or stays elevated but the 10-year yield falls over the next 20 trading days in a majority of cases, or if the conditional hit rate is not above 55% when CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating year over year.

mechanism_narrative: A low and falling UNRATE indicates tightening labor slack and stronger demand pressure, which pushes market participants to price a higher path for rates and causes the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below its 6-month moving average or drops by at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if labor-market tightening triggers do not coincide with a positive 20-day change in the 10-year yield, or if the signal loses sign consistency when PAYEMS growth is simultaneously positive.

mechanism_narrative: When RETAIL sales proxy RSAFS and industrial output INDPRO both soften at the same time, the market reads a broad demand slowdown and bids the 10-year yield lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration in RSAFS and INDPRO is not followed by lower 10-year yields in at least 55% of triggered episodes, or if either proxy alone explains the effect better than the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior slump signals re-acceleration in real activity and reduced recession risk, which pushes the 10-year yield up over a 60-day horizon as growth expectations reprice.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive months and its 3-month change turns positive
failure_condition: Reject if three-month industrial acceleration is not followed by a higher 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days, or if the conditional forward yield change is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates faster than FEDFUNDS adjusts, real rates compress and bond investors demand more nominal compensation, so the 10-year yield rises over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if inflation overshoots the policy rate but the 10-year yield does not rise in the next 20 trading days, or if the hit rate falls below 50% in periods of positive inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A strong housing start reading in HOUST transmits into firmer demand expectations and a higher expected path for rates, which lifts the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large positive HOUST surprises are followed by flat or lower 10-year yields more than half the time, or if the signal disappears once housing data are seasonally adjusted.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate combined with accelerating payroll growth signals labor-market tightening, which increases growth and inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops while PAYEMS rises in the same monthly release cycle
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous UNRATE declines and PAYEMS gains do not lead to a higher 10-year yield over the subsequent 5 trading days, or if the effect reverses during multiple consecutive release months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales RSAFS soften while GDP growth GDPC1 is still decelerating, investors infer an earnings and demand slowdown, which lowers risk appetite and pulls the 10-year yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior-quarter pace and RSAFS posts a negative monthly change
failure_condition: Reject if the joint slowdown pattern fails to produce lower 10-year yields in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the effect is absent when UNRATE is stable or rising.
